input_dict.question: The musician also known as "Braddah Iz"  is a member of what Hawaiian musical group?
input_dict.source_summary: Israel Kamakawiwoʻole (also called "Braddah Iz") was a founding member of the Mākaha Sons of Ni'ihau.
input_dict.target_summary: Israel Kamakawiwoʻole (also called "Braddah Iz") — identified in the first-hop context — is listed in the retrieved material as one of the founding members of The Mākaha Sons of Ni'ihau. The Mākaha Sons of Ni'ihau are a Hawaiian musical group formed in 1976 on the island of Oahu by Jerome Koko, Louis "Moon" Kauakahi, Skippy Kamakawiwo'ole, Israel Kamakawiwoʻole, and Sam Gray.
input_dict.candidate_summary: Israel Kamakawiwoʻole was a founding member of the Mākaha Sons of Ni'ihau, a Hawaiian musical group formed on the island of Oahu.
